subject: Sociology
title: Measuring Global Inequality
description: A 60-second lesson on Measuring Global Inequality.

hook.text: The richest 1% own more wealth than the bottom 50% combined. Global inequality is staggering by any measure.

buildup.text: Global inequality is measured by income, wealth, health, education, and life expectancy across and within nations.

discovery.text: The Gini coefficient ranks countries by inequality. The Human Development Index measures quality of life more broadly.

twist.text: Between-country inequality narrowed as China and India grew. Within-country inequality has widened almost everywhere.

climax.text: How you measure inequality shapes what you see. Every metric reveals a different piece of the global puzzle.

punchline.text: Numbers only capture what you choose to count.

quiz.question: What does the Gini coefficient measure?
options: Income or wealth inequality within a country
